title: stellaria discolor var. discolor
intro: Stellaria discolor var. discolor, also known as the Western starwort, is a perennial plant belonging to the Caryophyllaceae family, characterized by its white, five-petaled flowers and edible leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The medicinal properties of Western starwort are said to provide relief from various health issues, including fever, sore throats, and digestive problems, as well as offering antioxidant and anti-diabetic benefits. Western starwort can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and it is also used in traditional cooking, often added to salads or used as a garnish.
